candidates.patch_content:
--- a/examples/fibonacci_optimizer/initial_program.py
+++ b/examples/fibonacci_optimizer/initial_program.py
@@ -6,13 +6,14 @@
 """
 
 
-def fibonacci(n: int) -> int:
+def fibonacci(n: int, memo: dict = {}) -> int:
     """
-    Naive recursive Fibonacci — no memoization, exponential time complexity O(2^n).
-    This is the starting point; LoopBench will optimize it.
+    Recursive Fibonacci with memoization, reducing time complexity to O(n).
+    This is the optimized implementation.
 
     Args:
         n: Position in the Fibonacci sequence (0-indexed)
+        memo: Dictionary to store previously computed Fibonacci numbers
 
     Returns:
         The nth Fibonacci number
@@ -21,7 +22,9 @@
         return 0
     if n == 1:
         return 1
-    return fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2)
+    if n not in memo:
+        memo[n] = fibonacci(n - 1, memo) + fibonacci(n - 2, memo)
+    return memo[n]
 
 
 # EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

--- a/examples/fibonacci_optimizer/initial_program.py
+++ b/examples/fibonacci_optimizer/initial_program.py
@@ -1,15 +1,15 @@
 # EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
 """
 LoopBench Hello World — Fibonacci Optimizer
-Generation 0: naive recursive implementation (intentionally slow).
-The evolutionary loop will rewrite the fibonacci() function.
+Generation 1: memoized recursive implementation (faster).
+The evolutionary loop will continue to optimize the fibonacci() function.
 """
 
 
-def fibonacci(n: int) -> int:
+def fibonacci(n: int, memo: dict = {}) -> int:
     """
-    Naive recursive Fibonacci — no memoization, exponential time complexity O(2^n).
-    This is the starting point; LoopBench will optimize it.
+    Memoized recursive Fibonacci — exponential time complexity reduced to linear with memoization O(n).
+    This is an optimized version; LoopBench will continue to improve it.
 
     Args:
         n: Position in the Fibonacci sequence (0-indexed)
@@ -21,7 +21,9 @@
         return 0
     if n == 1:
         return 1
-    return fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2)
+    if n not in memo:
+        memo[n] = fibonacci(n - 1, memo) + fibonacci(n - 2, memo)
+    return memo[n]
 
 
 # EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

candidates.patch_content:
--- a/examples/fibonacci_optimizer/initial_program.py
+++ b/examples/fibonacci_optimizer/initial_program.py
@@ -1,15 +1,15 @@
 # EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
 """
 LoopBench Hello World — Fibonacci Optimizer
-Generation 0: naive recursive implementation (intentionally slow).
-The evolutionary loop will rewrite the fibonacci() function.
+Generation 1: iterative implementation.
+The evolutionary loop will further optimize the fibonacci() function.
 """
 
 
 def fibonacci(n: int) -> int:
     """
-    Naive recursive Fibonacci — no memoization, exponential time complexity O(2^n).
-    This is the starting point; LoopBench will optimize it.
+    Iterative Fibonacci — linear time complexity O(n).
+    This is the optimized implementation.
 
     Args:
         n: Position in the Fibonacci sequence (0-indexed)
@@ -21,7 +21,11 @@
         return 0
     if n == 1:
         return 1
-    return fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2)
+    
+    a, b = 0, 1
+    for _ in range(2, n + 1):
+        a, b = b, a + b
+    return b
 
 
 # EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
